title: Biden-Harris Administration Announces $635 Million in Awards to Continue Expanding Zero-Emission EV Charging and Refueling Infrastructure
content: The Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) announced $635 million in grants to fund 49 projects across 27 states, expanding EV charging and alternative fueling infrastructure under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law. These projects will add 11,500 EV charging ports and support hydrogen and natural gas fueling. The U.S. is on track to meet President Bidenâ€™s goal of 500,000 EV chargers by 2030, with 206,000 publicly available chargers already in place, including 38,000 new chargers added in 2024. The Bipartisan Infrastructure Lawâ€™s $2.5 billion Charging and Fueling Infrastructure (CFI) Grant Program and NEVI Formula Program have driven public and private investments, accelerating EV adoption, creating manufacturing jobs, and supporting a clean energy economy nationwide.
author: Analysts Account
